title: scaevola restiacea
intro: Scaevola restiacea, also known as the Australian fan flower, is a flowering plant native to the eastern coast of Australia. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Indigenous Australian culture, for its medicinal properties, which are said to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Scaevola restiacea include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves or roots and can be used as teas, infusions, or applied topically in the form of ointments or creams.
